General Dentist — focused on Restorative Dentistry

I care for teeth the way I'd want my own family's cared for: carefully diagnosed, cleanly restored, and explained in plain language along the way. Outside the clinic
Bioactive terrariums — 2 years

Restorative dentistry rewards patience and a steady hand — so does building a closed, living ecosystem. I've spent the past two years keeping bioactive terrariums, and this is my rain terrarium.

Bioactive means the terrarium is a genuine self-sustaining ecosystem, not just a planted tank — a cleanup crew of live organisms processes waste and breaks down old plant matter continuously, so the system essentially maintains itself with little to no manual upkeep.

It also runs smart: a smart switch and a set of sensors handle lighting and the rain/misting cycle automatically, on a schedule, with no manual intervention day to day.

The cleanup crew works in layers: isopods and springtails handle the upper, humid terrestrial zone, while snails and shrimp take care of the aquatic zone at the bottom.

The full setup — layered planting, natural rain cycle
